behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is pure backoff math/state progression. MOCK-MISS: this behavior can fail inside one function/object without module handoff. Rejection gate: not Integration/System because behavior text does not require real I/O, network, subprocess, or cross-module wiring to expose wrong doubling/cap logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test a backoff calculator/state object directly: feed consecutive failure counts and assert delays follow 1x,2x,4x... and clamp at max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between response classification and retry scheduler/wait computation. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only classifier or only calculator can miss wiring mistakes (e.g., wrong status/header mapped to backoff). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry policy + response parser + wait calculator in-process. Provide synthetic HTTP responses (e.g., 429 + headers) and assert computed next-wait passed into scheduler is correct.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction of timer/scheduler, retry orchestrator, and transport wrapper to prove resend of same request and transparent return. Rejection gate: not System because real network is not required; a deterministic in-process fake transport plus controllable clock can expose resend/forwarding bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that returns rate-limit then success; assert second attempt payload equals original request and `call()` resolves to the successful response value.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-loop termination + error translation across modules (policy, loop, public API). A single-function unit may miss where generic transport errors leak through orchestration. Rejection gate: not System/Agentic because behavior does not require real infra or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries, force repeated rate-limit responses from a stub transport, and assert `call()` rejects with `QuotaExhaustedError` type and expected metadata, not generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets end-to-end in-process orchestration across retry chain and shared quota state; infinite-loop risks usually emerge from module interaction/state handoff. Rejection gate: not System unless the loop bug depends on real timers/network behavior; behavior text does not require that. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run full client stack with fake clock and stub transport that keeps rate-limiting; assert bounded number of attempts, terminal error emitted, and test fails if attempt count/time exceeds strict guard threshold.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is shared-state coordination across concurrent callers and quota manager module; unit-testing one function misses race/handoff issues. Integration brake: not System because same-process async concurrency can be reproduced in-process without real network/OS primitives; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create one client instance with shared quota bucket and launch concurrent `call()` invocations against a stub transport; assert aggregate token/attempt accounting reflects one shared bucket, not per-caller independent quotas.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering guarantee depends on queue + dispatcher + retry gating interactions; cross-module orchestration failure boundary. Rejection gate: not System because FIFO dispatch correctness can be validated with in-process queue and deterministic scheduler/fake timers; no real network or subprocess needed.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue multiple pending calls with controlled release conditions in a single process, then assert dispatch sequence matches submission order under contention and retry waits.
